```diff
<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/sampler.h
+#pragma once
+
+#include <vector>
+#include <cstddef>
+#include <cstdint>
+
+class Sampler {
+  public:
+    void Init(uint32_t sample_rate) { sr_ = sample_rate; buffer_.reserve(sr_ * 4); }
+    void StartRec() { buffer_.clear(); rec_ = true; }
+    void StopRec() { rec_ = false; }
+    void StartPlay(float rate = 1.0f) { play_ = true; ph_ = 0.0f; inc_ = rate; }
+    void StopPlay() { play_ = false; }
+
+    void FeedInputBlock(const float* in, size_t n) {
+        if(!rec_) return;
+        buffer_.reserve(buffer_.size() + n);
+        for(size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) {
+            float x = in[i] * 32767.0f;
+            if(x > 32767.0f) x = 32767.0f;
+            if(x < -32768.0f) x = -32768.0f;
+            buffer_.push_back((int16_t)x);
+        }
+    }
+
+    float Next() {
+        if(!play_ || buffer_.empty()) return 0.0f;
+        uint32_t i = (uint32_t)ph_;
+        uint32_t j = (i + 1 < buffer_.size()) ? i + 1 : i;
+        float frac = ph_ - (float)i;
+        float a = (float)buffer_[i];
+        float b = (float)buffer_[j];
+        ph_ += inc_;
+        if(ph_ >= (float)buffer_.size()) play_ = false;
+        return ((a * (1.0f - frac) + b * frac) * (1.0f / 32767.0f));
+    }
+
+    void MakePreview(size_t start, size_t end, size_t decim, std::vector<int16_t>& out) const {
+        if(end > buffer_.size()) end = buffer_.size();
+        if(start > end) start = end;
+        out.clear();
+        if(decim == 0) decim = 1;
+        out.reserve((end - start) / decim + 1);
+        for(size_t i = start; i < end; i += decim) out.push_back(buffer_[i]);
+    }
+
+    static void BlockMeters(const float* in, size_t n, float& rms, float& peak) {
+        double acc = 0.0;
+        float pk = 0.0f;
+        for(size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) {
+            float x = in[i];
+            float ax = x < 0.0f ? -x : x;
+            acc += (double)x * (double)x;
+            if(ax > pk) pk = ax;
+        }
+        rms = n ? (float)sqrt(acc / (double)n) : 0.0f;
+        peak = pk;
+    }
+
+  private:
+    uint32_t sr_ = 48000;
+    std::vector<int16_t> buffer_;
+    bool rec_ = false;
+    bool play_ = false;
+    float ph_ = 0.0f;
+    float inc_ = 1.0f;
+};
+
*** End Patch
PATCH
```

```diff
<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/cv_bus.h
+#pragma once
+
+#include <cstdint>
+#include <array>
+#include "daisy_seed.h"
+
+struct CvCal {
+    float vcf_cut_gain = 1.0f, vcf_cut_off = 0.0f;
+    float vcf_q_gain = 1.0f, vcf_q_off = 0.0f;
+    float vca_gain = 1.0f, vca_off = 0.0f;
+    float cutoff_k = 3.0f; // exponential curvature
+};
+
+class CvBus {
+  public:
+    void Init(uint8_t i2c_addr = 0x60) {
+        addr_ = i2c_addr;
+        daisy::I2CHandle::Config cfg;
+        cfg.periph = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Peripheral::I2C_1;
+        cfg.mode = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Mode::I2C_MASTER;
+        cfg.speed = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Speed::I2C_400KHZ;
+        i2c_.Init(cfg);
+    }
+
+    void SetVoiceCal(uint8_t v, const CvCal& c) { if(v < cal_.size()) cal_[v] = c; }
+
+    // Normalized 0..1 controls
+    void QueueVoice(uint8_t v, float cutoff, float res, float vca) {
+        if(v >= max_voices_) return;
+        const auto& C = cal_[v];
+        auto clamp01 = [](float x){ return x < 0.0f ? 0.0f : (x > 1.0f ? 1.0f : x); };
+        auto shaped = [&](float x){
+            float e = expf(C.cutoff_k * x) - 1.0f;
+            float d = expf(C.cutoff_k) - 1.0f;
+            float y = d > 0.0f ? (e / d) : x;
+            return y;
+        };
+        float cut = clamp01(C.vcf_cut_gain * shaped(clamp01(cutoff)) + C.vcf_cut_off);
+        float q = clamp01(C.vcf_q_gain * clamp01(res) + C.vcf_q_off);
+        float vca_inv = 1.0f - clamp01(vca);
+        float va = clamp01(C.vca_gain * vca_inv + C.vca_off);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 0] = (uint16_t)lrintf(cut * 4095.0f);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 1] = (uint16_t)lrintf(q * 4095.0f);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 2] = (uint16_t)lrintf(va * 4095.0f);
+    }
+
+    void Flush() {
+        // Phase I: one MCP4728 (channels 0..2 used)
+        writeMCP4728_Quad(slot_[0], slot_[1], slot_[2], 0);
+    }
+
+  private:
+    void writeMCP4728_Quad(uint16_t ch0, uint16_t ch1, uint16_t ch2, uint16_t ch3) {
+        uint8_t buf[9];
+        size_t k = 0;
+        buf[k++] = 0x00; // Fast Write command
+        auto emit = [&](uint16_t v){
+            buf[k++] = (uint8_t)((v >> 8) & 0x0F);
+            buf[k++] = (uint8_t)(v & 0xFF);
+        };
+        emit(ch0); emit(ch1); emit(ch2); emit(ch3);
+        i2c_.TransmitBlocking(addr_ << 1, buf, k, 1);
+    }
+
+    uint8_t addr_ = 0x60;
+    daisy::I2CHandle i2c_;
+    static constexpr uint8_t max_voices_ = 8;
+    std::array<CvCal, max_voices_> cal_{};
+    std::array<uint16_t, max_voices_ * 3> slot_{};
+};
+
*** End Patch
PATCH
```

```diff
<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/sampler.hpp
+#pragma once
+
+#include <vector>
+#include <cstddef>
+#include <cstdint>
+#include <stdint.h>
+#include <math.h>
+
+class Sampler {
+  public:
+    void Init(uint32_t sample_rate) { sr_ = sample_rate; buffer_.reserve(sr_ * 4); }
+    void StartRec() { buffer_.clear(); rec_ = true; }
+    void StopRec() { rec_ = false; }
+    void StartPlay(float rate = 1.0f) { play_ = true; ph_ = 0.0f; inc_ = rate; }
+    void StopPlay() { play_ = false; }
+
+    void FeedInputBlock(const float* in, size_t n) {
+        if(!rec_) return;
+        buffer_.reserve(buffer_.size() + n);
+        for(size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) {
+            float x = in[i] * 32767.0f;
+            if(x > 32767.0f) x = 32767.0f;
+            if(x < -32768.0f) x = -32768.0f;
+            buffer_.push_back((int16_t)x);
+        }
+    }
+
+    float Next() {
+        if(!play_ || buffer_.empty()) return 0.0f;
+        uint32_t i = (uint32_t)ph_;
+        uint32_t j = (i + 1 < buffer_.size()) ? i + 1 : i;
+        float frac = ph_ - (float)i;
+        float a = (float)buffer_[i];
+        float b = (float)buffer_[j];
+        ph_ += inc_;
+        if(ph_ >= (float)buffer_.size()) play_ = false;
+        return ((a * (1.0f - frac) + b * frac) * (1.0f / 32767.0f));
+    }
+
+    void MakePreview(size_t start, size_t end, size_t decim, std::vector<int16_t>& out) const {
+        if(end > buffer_.size()) end = buffer_.size();
+        if(start > end) start = end;
+        out.clear();
+        if(decim == 0) decim = 1;
+        out.reserve((end - start) / decim + 1);
+        for(size_t i = start; i < end; i += decim) out.push_back(buffer_[i]);
+    }
+
+    static void BlockMeters(const float* in, size_t n, float& rms, float& peak) {
+        double acc = 0.0;
+        float pk = 0.0f;
+        for(size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) {
+            float x = in[i];
+            float ax = x < 0.0f ? -x : x;
+            acc += (double)x * (double)x;
+            if(ax > pk) pk = ax;
+        }
+        rms = n ? (float)sqrt(acc / (double)n) : 0.0f;
+        peak = pk;
+    }
+
+  private:
+    uint32_t sr_ = 48000;
+    std::vector<int16_t> buffer_;
+    bool rec_ = false;
+    bool play_ = false;
+    float ph_ = 0.0f;
+    float inc_ = 1.0f;
+};
+
*** End Patch
```

```diff
<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/src/cv_bus.hpp
+#pragma once
+
+#include <cstdint>
+#include <stdint.h>
+#include <array>
+#include <math.h>
+#include "daisy_seed.h"
+
+struct CvCal {
+    float vcf_cut_gain = 1.0f, vcf_cut_off = 0.0f;
+    float vcf_q_gain = 1.0f, vcf_q_off = 0.0f;
+    float vca_gain = 1.0f, vca_off = 0.0f;
+    float cutoff_k = 3.0f;
+};
+
+class CvBus {
+  public:
+    void Init(uint8_t i2c_addr = 0x60) {
+        addr_ = i2c_addr;
+        daisy::I2CHandle::Config cfg;
+        cfg.periph = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Peripheral::I2C_1;
+        cfg.mode = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Mode::I2C_MASTER;
+        cfg.speed = daisy::I2CHandle::Config::Speed::I2C_400KHZ;
+        i2c_.Init(cfg);
+    }
+
+    void SetVoiceCal(uint8_t v, const CvCal& c) { if(v < cal_.size()) cal_[v] = c; }
+
+    void QueueVoice(uint8_t v, float cutoff, float res, float vca) {
+        if(v >= max_voices_) return;
+        const auto& C = cal_[v];
+        auto clamp01 = [](float x){ return x < 0.0f ? 0.0f : (x > 1.0f ? 1.0f : x); };
+        auto shaped = [&](float x){
+            float e = expf(C.cutoff_k * x) - 1.0f;
+            float d = expf(C.cutoff_k) - 1.0f;
+            float y = d > 0.0f ? (e / d) : x;
+            return y;
+        };
+        float cut = clamp01(C.vcf_cut_gain * shaped(clamp01(cutoff)) + C.vcf_cut_off);
+        float q = clamp01(C.vcf_q_gain * clamp01(res) + C.vcf_q_off);
+        float vca_inv = 1.0f - clamp01(vca);
+        float va = clamp01(C.vca_gain * vca_inv + C.vca_off);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 0] = (uint16_t)lrintf(cut * 4095.0f);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 1] = (uint16_t)lrintf(q * 4095.0f);
+        slot_[v * 3 + 2] = (uint16_t)lrintf(va * 4095.0f);
+    }
+
+    void Flush() { writeMCP4728_Quad(slot_[0], slot_[1], slot_[2], 0); }
+
+  private:
+    void writeMCP4728_Quad(uint16_t ch0, uint16_t ch1, uint16_t ch2, uint16_t ch3) {
+        uint8_t buf[9];
+        size_t k = 0;
+        buf[k++] = 0x00; // Fast Write
+        auto emit = [&](uint16_t v){
+            buf[k++] = (uint8_t)((v >> 8) & 0x0F);
+            buf[k++] = (uint8_t)(v & 0xFF);
+        };
+        emit(ch0); emit(ch1); emit(ch2); emit(ch3);
+        i2c_.TransmitBlocking((uint16_t)(addr_ << 1), buf, k, 1);
+    }
+
+    uint8_t addr_ = 0x60;
+    daisy::I2CHandle i2c_;
+    static constexpr uint8_t max_voices_ = 8;
+    std::array<CvCal, max_voices_> cal_{};
+    std::array<uint16_t, max_voices_ * 3> slot_{};
+};
+
*** End Patch
PATCH
```

It looks like the linter tool might be running a C linter, since I’m seeing errors like "namespace std has no member vector.” This suggests it's not configured for C++. Also, it’s giving generic linting errors and seems to be flagging issues in our new files before the build has been run. I notice that header files should generally not be listed as sources in CMake, though including them is fine. The linter may indeed be expecting pure C, which could explain the confusion.
**Examining firmware linter issues**

The Daisy firmware is using C++, specifically daisysp. I think the linter might require guards for proper includes. The instructions suggest that we should run the linter and fix any clear errors. I wonder if using .cpp implementation files instead of header-only files will help the linter. It seems the linter may be set for C, as indicated by complaints about std::vector. If we convert headers to .cpp and .h files with C declarations, that could help avoid linter issues, but it still might flag things.
**Preparing for the build**

I need to follow the developer tool policy that states I should run tests and builds before marking something complete. I can do this by executing the `bash build.sh` script using `run_terminal_cmd`. However, the devcontainer requires the IDF_PATH to be set; otherwise, the build script will error. To simplify, I can focus on building just the Daisy part by running cmake and make in the firmware/daisy/build directory. I'll make sure to check that CMake includes C++17 first, as we configured this in CMakeLists.
**Clarifying include paths and updates**

I’ve made a change to the shared header directory, ensuring the include path is set to one level up and correctly includes "../shared." The protocol header path "spi_protocol/protocol.h" is used in main.cpp, while the CMake configuration adds the "shared" path. This setup seems right since the header is actually located in "firmware/shared/spi_protocol/protocol.h." I also need to link the Daisy libraries from the existing libs directories. Additionally, I updated main.cpp to implement logic for a 1 kHz tick, ensuring it runs every callback correctly.
**Preparing to build the project**

I noticed we used `tanhf`, so I’m considering whether to include `<cmath>` in main. Since it’s already included in the Daisy libraries, it might not be necessary, but including it could be a safe choice. Now, I need to build everything. I also realized we kept the existing HAL SPI code; that’s not relevant to the linter. I’ll propose the build command using `functions.run_terminal_cmd` to create the build directory and run cmake and make without using a pager. Let’s execute that command!</details></think>

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/daisy/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.15)
 
 # Set the project name
 set(PROJECT_NAME wavex-daisy)
 project(${PROJECT_NAME} C CXX ASM)
 
 # Use C++17 for modern headers and defaults
 set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
 set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
+set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
+set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
+
+# Ensure STM32 device macros are visible to all subdirs (HAL expects these)
+add_definitions(
+    -DSTM32H750xx
+    -DUSE_HAL_DRIVER
+    -DHSE_VALUE=16000000
+    -DHSI_VALUE=16000000
+    -DARM_MATH_CM7
+)
@@
-# Add libDaisy and DaisySP
-add_subdirectory(libs/libDaisy)
-add_subdirectory(libs/DaisySP)
+# Add libDaisy and DaisySP
+add_subdirectory(libs/libDaisy)
+add_subdirectory(libs/DaisySP)
*** End Patch
PATCH
```

Model: GPT-5 Code, 32k, Assistant, rev 2025-08-13

Quick status: I reproduced the errors with the CMake build, switched to the libDaisy Makefile (known-good for HAL config), and the Daisy firmware now builds cleanly.

What I did
- Used the Daisy Makefile instead of CMake to avoid STM32 HAL config issues.
- Integrated Phase 1 scaffolding:
  - Added `sampler` and `cv_bus` modules and a simple `timebase`.
  - Wired them into `firmware/daisy/src/main.cpp` with 48-sample blocks (1 kHz tick), sampler feed/playback mix, and CV queue/flush.
- Confirmed successful build.

How to build locally
```bash
cd firmware/daisy
make clean && make -j
```

Artifacts
- Daisy ELF/HEX/BIN are in `firmware/daisy/build/`
  - `wavex-daisy.bin` is ready for flashing.

Notes
- The earlier failures were from the CMake path misconfiguring STM32 HAL/LL macros for H7; libDaisy’s Makefile sets the correct flags/includes for Daisy hardware.
- The `.hpp` headers (`sampler.hpp`, `cv_bus.hpp`, `timebase.hpp`) are used by `main.cpp`. Old `.h` variants exist but aren’t compiled.

Next steps I can take
- Hook sampler record/play and CV params to SPI protocol messages (PARAM_SET, SAMPLE_CTRL).
- Add meter/preview push cadence at 1 kHz and IRQ flow integration.
- Optionally remove the unused `.h` duplicates to avoid confusion.

Summary
- Switched Daisy build to Makefile; build now succeeds.
- Implemented Phase 1 glue: 48-sample blocks, sampler mix, CV queue/flush.
- Output at `firmware/daisy/build/wavex-daisy.bin`; ready to flash.

```diff
+    float peak;
```

```diff
 }
+
+bool ProtocolHandler::ParseSampleCtrl(const uint8_t* buffer, SampleCtrlMessage& msg)
+{
+    const Packet* packet = reinterpret_cast<const Packet*>(buffer);
+    if (packet->header.type != MSG_SAMPLE_CTRL || packet->header.length != sizeof(SampleCtrlMessage)) {
+        return false;
+    }
+    memcpy(&msg, packet->payload, sizeof(SampleCtrlMessage));
+    return true;
+}
+
+bool ProtocolHandler::ParsePreviewReq(const uint8_t* buffer, PreviewReqMessage& msg)
+{
+    const Packet* packet = reinterpret_cast<const Packet*>(buffer);
+    if (packet->header.type != MSG_PREVIEW_REQ || packet->header.length != sizeof(PreviewReqMessage)) {
+        return false;
+    }
+    memcpy(&msg, packet->payload, sizeof(PreviewReqMessage));
+    return true;
+}
*** End Patch
```

```diff
+    p->header.type = type;
```

```diff
@@
-SPI_HandleTypeDef hspi1;
-uint8_t spi_rx_buffer[128];
-uint8_t spi_tx_buffer[128];
-volatile bool spi_data_ready = false;
+SPI_HandleTypeDef hspi1;
+uint8_t spi_rx_buffer[128];
+uint8_t spi_tx_buffer[128];
+uint8_t spi_dummy_rx[128];
+volatile bool spi_data_ready = false;
+static volatile bool g_tx_inflight = false;
+static uint16_t g_tx_len = 0;
@@
-static void SendPacket(uint8_t type, const void* payload, size_t length)
+static void SendPacket(uint8_t type, const void* payload, size_t length)
 {
     if(length > MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE) length = MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE;
     Packet* p = reinterpret_cast<Packet*>(spi_tx_buffer);
     p->header.sync = SYNC_BYTE;
     p->header.type = type;
     p->header.length = (uint8_t)length;
     if(payload && length)
         memcpy(p->payload, payload, length);
     p->header.checksum = ProtocolHandler::CalculateChecksum(p->payload, p->header.length);
-    g_tx_ready = true;
-    IRQ_Raise();
+    g_tx_ready = true;
+    g_tx_len = (uint16_t)(sizeof(PacketHeader) + p->header.length);
+    // Prime TXRX so next master clock will shift the packet out
+    if(!g_tx_inflight) {
+        if(HAL_SPI_TransmitReceive_IT(&hspi1, spi_tx_buffer, spi_dummy_rx, g_tx_len) == HAL_OK) {
+            g_tx_inflight = true;
+            IRQ_Raise();
+        }
+    }
 }
*** End Patch
```
